sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Agree a new Net Change Limit for the Cardano Treasury for Epochs 613-713 through an Info action with no direct on-chain effect and no authorization of any Treasury Withdrawal.
Stated amount: 500,000,000 ada net cap on total Treasury Withdrawals during the period; no withdrawal amount requested

Deliverables: Record DReps' agreement to a 500,000,000 ada Net Change Limit for Epochs 613-713, Supersede any prior Net Change Limit for the same period, Count all Treasury Withdrawals already debited during the period toward the limit, Apply a cumulative lovelace accounting method that excludes Treasury inflows
Deadline/expiry: period begins at the start of Epoch 613 on February 13, 2026 and ends at the close of Epoch 713 on or about July 3, 2027

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal sets a new Net Change Limit of 500,000,000 ada, equal to 500,000,000,000,000 lovelace, for the Cardano Treasury during Epochs 613-713.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The Net Change Limit caps only the cumulative lovelace removed from the Treasury through enacted Treasury Withdrawals recorded on the Cardano ledger during the period.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Treasury inflows, including reserve and protocol allocations, transaction-fee allocations, refunds, and returns of Treasury-funded amounts, do not count toward or increase the limit.
Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Treasury Withdrawals already debited during the Epoch 613-713 period count toward the 500,000,000 ada limit and remain valid.
Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal states that this Info action has no direct on-chain effect and does not authorize any Treasury Withdrawal.

Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposer asserts that the existing 350,000,000 ada Net Change Limit has served as a useful fiscal guardrail but is becoming practically constrained before mid-2027.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposer asserts that the existing 350,000,000 ada Net Change Limit has served as a useful fiscal guardrail but is becoming practically constrained before mid-2027.

Independent evidence for: The proposer claims the 500,000,000 ada limit preserves DReps' ability to consider credible Treasury Withdrawals on their merits while retaining a fixed guardrail and simple accounting.
Independent evidence for: The proposal states that Yes votes exceeding 50% of active voting stake record DRep agreement to the Net Change Limit under threshold TREASURY-01a.
Independent evidence for: The proposer claims nothing in the Constitution limits DReps to a single Net Change Limit or prevents a newly agreed Net Change Limit from applying to the same period.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal sets a new Net Change Limit of 500,000,000 ada, equal to 500,000,000,000,000 lovelace, for the Cardano Treasury during Epochs 613-713." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposer asserts that the existing 350,000,000 ada Net Change Limit has served as a useful fiscal guardrail but is becoming practically constrained before mid-2027." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
